purpose: This repository contains Apache Airflow DAGs (Directed Acyclic Graphs) that orchestrate various ETL processes for a loan selling and servicing platform. It automates data ingestion, transformation, and synchronization between systems to enable loan management, fee collection, interest accrual, and reporting.
businessFeatures: Loan data ingestion and curation from source systems
Data export for reporting and analytics
SOFR rate ingestion for interest calculations

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core loan data representing originated loans in the system

dataEntities.name: Fee
dataEntities.description: Fee data for loan processing and servicing

dataEntities.name: MPL
dataEntities.description: Marketplace Lender entities that originate loans

dataEntities.name: Organization
dataEntities.description: Issuing banks and investors that participate in the lending ecosystem

dataEntities.name: SOFR
dataEntities.description: Secured Overnight Financing Rate data used for interest calculations

findings.title: Secret Management Inconsistency
findings.description: The repository uses a mix of AWS Secrets Manager and environment variables for credential management. Some credentials like database passwords are properly managed through Secrets Manager ARNs, while others may be exposed through environment files (e.g., selling.env). All secrets should be managed consistently through Secrets Manager to prevent potential credential exposure.

findings.title: Missing Error Handling in Dag Tasks
findings.description: Many of the DAG tasks have minimal or no error handling, which can lead to silent failures or incomplete executions without clear diagnostics. Tasks should implement proper exception handling with appropriate logging and recovery mechanisms to ensure reliability.
